content:
Your Folder Structure Says More About Your Architecture Than You Think

4 min read
3 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

One of the first things developers notice when opening an Angular project is the folder structure.
Some projects look like this:
src/
├── components/
├── services/
├── models/
├── pipes/
├── guards/
├── directives/Others look completely different:
src/
├── features/
│ ├── customers/
│ ├── orders/
│ ├── reports/
│ ├── dashboard/Both applications can work.
Both compile.
Both pass tests.
Yet one usually scales much better.
Why?
Because folder structure isn’t about organizing files.
It’s about organizing ownership.
We Don’t Build Components
When developers first learn Angular, they often organize projects by file type.
Components together.
Services together.
Pipes together.
Guards together.
